question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: processTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a PublishRequest with sendRecord=true to force a hardcoded Hbar.from(1, HbarUnit.HBAR) query payment per TransactionRecordQuery execution, draining the operator account by flooding the publish() endpoint with high-volume record-fetch requests, causing direct HBAR loss from the operator wallet?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: processTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged user trigger repeated calls to publish() with sendRecord=true, where each call unconditionally executes a TransactionRecordQuery with setQueryPayment(Hbar.from(1, HbarUnit.HBAR)) without any per-request budget cap or rate limit, causing cumulative 1 HBAR deductions per request and draining the operator account to zero?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged user submit an unbounded stream of PublishRequest objects to publish() since there is no per-user rate limiting or total-spend guard, causing the operator account to pay transaction fees for every submitted transaction and resulting in complete fund exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged user supply a PublishRequest with a pre-set node (node != null) that bypasses the nodeSupplier.get() validation path, routing the operator-signed transaction to an arbitrary or attacker-controlled node account, causing the operator to pay fees to an illegitimate node and lose funds?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: toClient()] Can an unprivileged user who influences the node list (e.g., via a poisoned address book) cause toClient() to call client.setOperator(operatorId, operatorPrivateKey) with a network map pointing to attacker-controlled endpoints, so that all operator-signed transactions are submitted to attacker nodes and fees are collected by the attacker?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: toClient()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Collectors.toMap merge function (a, b) -> a in toClient() to cause duplicate node endpoints to silently drop legitimate nodes, reducing the effective network to a single attacker-controlled node that collects all operator transaction fees?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getClients()] Can an unprivileged user who can influence nodeSupplier.refresh() output cause getClients() to build a Flux<Client> cached via .cache() that permanently routes all operator transactions to attacker-controlled nodes, resulting in persistent fund loss across all future publish() calls for the lifetime of the application?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of a maximum-spend guard in publish() to submit transactions with arbitrarily large transfer amounts (e.g., CryptoTransfer of the operator's entire balance), causing direct and complete loss of all operator funds in a single transaction?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: processTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged user set both sendRecord=true and receipt=true in a PublishRequest to trigger multiple fee-bearing queries (TransactionRecordQuery + TransactionReceiptQuery) per transaction, multiplying the HBAR cost per publish() call and accelerating operator fund drain?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the lack of transaction type validation in publish() to submit a high-value CryptoTransfer transaction signed by the operator, transferring HBAR from the operator account to an attacker-controlled account, causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: execute(Client, Transaction)] Can an unprivileged user trigger the async execution path (publishProperties.isAsync()==true) in execute() to fire off a large number of concurrent operator-signed transactions via Mono.fromFuture(executable.executeAsync(client)), overwhelming the operator account with parallel fee deductions before any rate limit or balance check can intervene?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: execute(Client, Query)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the async query execution path in execute(Client, Query) to fire off many concurrent TransactionRecordQuery calls each paying 1 HBAR, with no concurrency cap, draining the operator account through parallel query payments?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of a minimum balance check before publish() executes a transaction, causing the operator account to go below the minimum HBAR reserve required by the network, resulting in account deletion or inability to pay future fees?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: toClient()] Can an unprivileged user who can inject a NodeProperties entry with a crafted certHash (null or empty ByteString) into the node list cause toClient() to build a NodeAddressBook with no certificate validation, enabling a man-in-the-middle to intercept operator-signed transactions and redirect funds?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getClients()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Flux.cache() on the clients field to poison the client pool once (e.g., via a malicious node during initial startup) and have all subsequent publish() calls permanently use the poisoned client, causing all operator transactions to be routed to attacker-controlled nodes and all fees lost?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged user set scenario.getProperties().getTimeout() to a very large value to prevent the timeout() operator from cancelling long-running transactions, allowing the operator to be locked into paying fees for transactions that are intentionally delayed by a malicious node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: processTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged user craft a PublishRequest where sendRecord=true and the underlying transaction is a no-op or already-failed transaction, causing the operator to pay 1 HBAR for a TransactionRecordQuery that returns no useful data, effectively burning funds with no legitimate purpose?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the lack of transaction amount validation in publish() to submit a TokenTransfer or CryptoTransfer that moves the operator's entire HBAR balance to an attacker account, with the operator key signing the transaction as part of normal publish flow?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: toClient()] Can an unprivileged user who controls the REST API address book response (via DNS poisoning or MITM of the mirror node REST endpoint) cause getAddressBook() to return attacker-controlled NodeProperties, which are then used in toClient() to build clients that route all operator funds to attacker nodes?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the onErrorMap() in publish() which wraps all non-PublishException errors into PublishException without halting execution, allowing a flood of failing transactions to still deduct fees from the operator account before the error is surfaced?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged user supply a PublishRequest with a Transaction<?> that has a maxTransactionFee set to an extremely high value, causing the operator to pay an inflated fee when the transaction is executed via execute(client, transaction), resulting in disproportionate fund loss per transaction?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the secureRandom.nextInt(publishProperties.getClients()) client selection in publish() to statistically target a specific client (e.g., one with a higher balance or different operator key) by sending many requests and observing which client processes them, then focusing attacks on that client?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: processTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that TransactionRecordQuery.setQueryPayment(Hbar.from(1, HbarUnit.HBAR)) is hardcoded and never validated against the operator's current balance, causing the operator to attempt a 1 HBAR payment even when the account balance is insufficient, triggering failed transactions that still consume network fees?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: toClient()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of TLS certificate pinning in toClient() (certHash is optional and can be empty) to perform a man-in-the-middle attack on the gRPC connection, intercepting operator-signed transactions and replaying or modifying them to redirect funds?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of a per-scenario spending limit in publish() to run a scenario with type=CRYPTO_TRANSFER and tps set to maximum, causing the operator to submit thousands of fund-transferring transactions per second until the account is drained?
